title: Smoky Silky Greek Whole Wheat Stuffed Grape Leaves
description: A twist on traditional dolmades, featuring whole wheat and yuzu-cure for a smoky, silky flavor.
instructions: Rinse the grape leaves and blanch them in boiling water for 30 seconds to remove bitterness.
Chop the onion, garlic, and leek, and saut8 them in olive oil until softened.
Add the ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
In a separate bowl, mix the cooked whole wheat berries with yuzu juice and a pinch of salt.
To assemble the dolmades, lay a grape leaf flat on a work surface, with the stem end facing you.
Place a tablespoon of the beef mixture at the stem end, and a tablespoon of the wheat mixture at the opposite end.
Fold the stem end over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the leaf into a neat package.
Repeat with the remaining grape leaves and filling ingredients.
Place the dolmades seam-side down in a steamer basket, and steam for 15-20 minutes, or until the grape leaves are tender.
Serve hot, garnished with lemon wedges and a sprinkle of sumac, if desired.
Refrigerate leftovers for up to 3 days, or freeze for up to 2 months.
When reheating, add a splash of water to the steamer basket and cover with a lid to restore moisture.
You can also reheat in a saucepan with a tablespoon of olive oil, or in the oven at 3504f for 10-15 minutes, or until heated through.
Serve with a dollop of tzatziki sauce, if desired.
For a more indulgent treat, serve with a side of crispy falafel and a sprinkle of sumac.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
